## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do and why? Link the related issue or plan/PHASES/ doc. -->

Fixes #
Related plan phase:

## Type of change

- [ ] feat — new user-visible feature
- [ ] fix — bug fix
- [ ] refactor — internal change, no behavioural diff
- [ ] perf — performance improvement
- [ ] docs — documentation only
- [ ] test — tests only
- [ ] chore / ci — tooling, build, CI
- [ ] BREAKING CHANGE — requires a major version bump

## Checklist

- [ ] Branch is up to date with `main` and has a linear history (rebase, no merge commits)
- [ ] Commits are signed (`git log --show-signature` shows `Good signature`)
- [ ] Conventional commit prefix in the PR title (`feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:` …)
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build && pnpm test` pass locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests for the change
- [ ] Added a changeset (`pnpm changeset`) for any user-visible change
- [ ] Updated docs under `docs/` or relevant README if behaviour changed

## Security & supply-chain impact

<!-- Required for any change to:
       - cryptographic signing / hashing of ZATCA XML
       - certificate, private key, or secret handling
       - HTTP request paths, headers, or TLS config
       - dependencies (added, removed, version-bumped)
       - GitHub Actions workflows or release tooling
     If none of the above, write "No security impact."
-->
